mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window or positive returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, which forces equity valuations down over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65% of instances where the unemployment threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP growth and stable consumer price inflation indicates robust aggregate demand without monetary policy tightening pressure, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 55% of occurrences or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical edge with p-value greater than 0.10 during recovery phases or Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.0%, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to compress equity multiples and reduce corporate profit margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in greater than 60% of months where the rate threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict return direction with greater than 55% accuracy or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in advance retail sales figures indicate expanding consumer demand, which translates directly into higher corporate revenue forecasts and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window or positive returns occur in less than 50% of rising retail sales months.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0% signals a sudden contraction in manufacturing activity and supply chain disruption, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65% of instances where the production decline threshold is met.
